description: Reference percentile tiers for golf prediction models, used to position BBI in the global landscape. Estimates based on publicly observable Spearman performance across published forecasters; closed proprietary models are inferred from research-paper benchmarks and tournament-pool track records.

current_state.industry_baseline_source: Published industry pre-tournament odds, mean of 62 events across 2025 + completed 2026 (PROMETHEUS backtest, refreshed weekly)

head_to_head_record_vs_baseline.note: Sample too small for confident verdict; 2026 baseline comparison is being computed only on majors and selected events. Real head-to-head will firm up over the season.

current_state.honest_summary: BBI averages 0.43 Spearman across 2026's 5 scored events, ranging from 0.34 (RBC Heritage) to 0.57 (Valero). That's measurably ahead of the 0.395 industry baseline (mean across 62 historical events from 2025-2026) — putting us in roughly the 88th-94th percentile of golf forecasting approaches. We're not yet best-in-world (0.55+ sustained), but we're playing in the top-tier commercial bracket from week one.

tiers.label: Noise / random
tiers.examples: Coin-flip rankings, social-media 'gut feel' picks, hot-hand fallacies
tiers.description: The unconditional baseline — picking by name recognition or recency. Half of all 'predictions' fall here.

tiers.label: Casual stats blogs
tiers.examples: Single-factor regressions, OWGR-only rankings, basic recent-form heuristics
tiers.description: Better than nothing — captures the broad-strokes tier of player quality but no edge on close decisions.

tiers.label: Solid public models
tiers.examples: Multi-factor regressions, modest course-fit, Strokes Gained baselines
tiers.description: Real signal. The kind of model a careful DFS player builds with public data.

tiers.label: Top-tier commercial
tiers.examples: Industry pre-tournament odds, paid analytics shops, professional sportsbook traders
tiers.description: The bar BBI competes against every week. Industry baseline season averages live here.

tiers.label: Best-in-world
tiers.examples: Closed proprietary research shops, top university statistics groups, sportsbook internals on majors
tiers.description: Sustained 0.50+ Spearman across full seasons. A handful of names in the world reach this consistently.

industry_baseline_marker.label: Industry baseline — 62-event multi-season backtest
industry_baseline_marker.note: Mean across 47 events of 2025 + 15 events of completed 2026, refreshed every Sunday

transparency_notes: Percentile estimates are derived from publicly observable model performance across forecasters. Where private models exist, we infer their tier from research papers and tournament-pool track records.
Spearman is one of several metrics; calibration (Brier score) and top-N hit rates are tracked separately on this page.
We refuse to make 'best-in-world' claims we can't substantiate. The marker reflects current 2026 season averages, not a single hot week.
